biological_role: Protein phosphatase involved in stress-signaling regulation
why_highlighted: The paper discusses PP2C-type phosphatases as regulators of stress-activated kinase cascades and notes previous links to herbivore defense.
Ethylene is an important phytohormone in plant stress and defense signaling, and this gene was highlighted from the binary-trait GWAS results.
Flavonoids can contribute to plant defense chemistry, and this gene was highlighted from the binary-trait analysis.
The paper highlights CRK3 as a strong defense-related candidate connected to calcium signaling and herbivore-responsive pathways.
The paper links the ACA12 region to calcium homeostasis and herbivore defense responses.
